# Oracle EBS 12.2 Connector - Full Documentation

# Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2 Connector 2.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Oracle EBS 12.2 Connector v2.3

Anypoint Connector for Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2 (EBS 12.2 Connector) facilitates connections
between Mule runtime engine (Mule) integration and Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2 applications.

## About the Connector Architecture

Oracle EBS offers different technologies or products to address various types of integrations. The
most widely used integrations for enterprise applications are:

- Data-centric integration
- Integration through Web Services using Oracle E-Business Suite Integrated SOA Gateway (ISG)

The following illustration shows the integration architecture of the MuleSoft Oracle EBS connector:

![Oracle EBS 12.2 Connector Architecture](_images/oracle-ebs122-architecture.png)

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- Oracle E-Business Suite
- Web services and integration scenarios for your Oracle EBS version
- Anypoint Connectors
- Anypoint Studio
- Mule concepts
- Elements in a Mule flow and global elements

You must also have:

- Oracle EBS login credentials to test your connection to your target resource
- Anypoint Studio
- Web service configuration in Oracle EBS

  Configuring Oracle EBS 12.2 Connector requires configuring REST web services in Oracle EBS and
  exposing them via the Integration Repository. For more information about how to configure Oracle
  EBS, refer to [To Use the Connector](#to-use-the-connector).

- FND_WEB_SEC PL/SQL Service

  The connector requires Oracle EBS to have the FND_WEB_SEC PL/SQL service deployed.

- REST Service Locator

  The connector requires Oracle EBS to have the REST Service Locator deployed as a REST service.

- WADL names in lower case

  WADLs corresponding to deployed PL/SQLs must have their internal names in lower case as an alias.
  For example, to deploy `PLSQL Interface : Absence Attendance Type`, which has
  `HR_ABSENCE_TYPE_API` as the internal name, its service alias must be `hr_absence_type_api`.

For software requirements and compatibility information, see the Connector Release Notes.

## POM File Information

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-oracle-ebs-122-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information, access the connector in
[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/) and click **Dependency Snippets**.

## Add the Connector to a Studio Project

Anypoint Studio provides two ways to add the connector to your Studio project:

- From the Exchange button in the Studio taskbar
- From the Mule Palette view

### Add the Connector Using Exchange

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  Click the Exchange icon **(X)** in the upper-left of the Studio task bar.
3.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
4.  In Exchange, search for "ebs".
5.  Select the connector and click **Add to project**.
6.  Follow the prompts to install the connector.

### Add the Connector in Studio

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the Mule Palette view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type "ebs" in the search field.
4.  Click this connectorâs name in **Available modules**.
5.  Click **Add**.
6.  Click **Finish**.

## To Use the Connector

1.  Deploy the REST Service location so that you can later expose your PL/SQL services for the
    connector to consume. See the Oracle Developerâs Guide Tutorial link in the See Also section.

    The following steps describe the procedure to deploy the PL/SQL Adjustment API as a REST
    service. The same guide applies to any PL/SQL.

2.  Log in to the Oracle E-Business Suite as a user who has the Integration Administrator role.
3.  Select the Integrated SOA Gateway responsibility and the Integration Repository link from the
    navigation menu.
4.  In the Integration Repository tab, click **Search** to access the main Search page.
5.  Click **Show More Search Options** to display more search fields.
6.  Enter the following key search values as the search criteria:
    - Category: `Interface Subtype`
    - Category Value: `PL/SQL`
    - Internal Name: `FA_ADJUSTMENT_PUB`

      ![Deploy PL/SQL Search page](_images/oracle-ebs122-deploy-plsql-1.png)

7.  Click **Go** to execute the search.
8.  Click the **Adjustments API** link to see the interface details.

    ![Deploy PL/SQL Adjustment API page](_images/oracle-ebs122-deploy-plsql-2.png)

9.  Click the REST Service Locator interface name link to open the interface details page.
10. In the REST Web Service tab, enter the following information:

    ![Deploy PL/SQL Adjustment API configuration](_images/oracle-ebs122-deploy-plsql-3.png)

    **Important:** The alias of the deployed Web Service MUST be:
    1.  The internal name.
    2.  In lowercase. The alias information can be found under the Service Alias label. In this
        example, it is fa_adjustment_pub.

11. Click **Deploy** to deploy the service to an Oracle E-Business Suite WebLogic environment.

    After the REST service successfully deploys, `Deployed` appears in the REST Service Status field
    along with the View WADL link.

12. Click the **View WADL** link to view the deployed service WADL description.

    ![Deploy PL/SQL Adjustment API deployed](_images/oracle-ebs122-deploy-plsql-4.png)

## To Configure in Studio

1.  Drag a connector operation to the Anypoint Studio canvas.
2.  Configure the Global element for the connector.

    ![Global elements properties window](_images/oracle-ebs122-config-studio11.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Host of the Oracle EBS instance</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the Oracle EBS Web Services</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SSL enabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>If checked, the connector makes Web Services calls using HTTPS instead of HTTP</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Username to log in to Oracle EBS Web Services</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Password for the username</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Rest Service Locator alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name assigned to the Web Service with the internal name <code>oracle.apps.fnd.rep.ws.service.EbsRestLocator</code> when deployed</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>FND Web Sec alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name assigned to the PL/SQL Web Service with the internal name <code>FND_WEB_SEC</code> when deployed</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Responsibility name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Oracle EBS responsibility name that is needed to execute the operation. This field accepts <code>responsibility_key</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Responsibility application name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Application short name that is needed to execute the operation</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security group name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Security group key of the Oracle EBS instance (<code>STANDARD</code> by default)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>NLS language</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) NLS language of the Oracle EBS instance (<code>AMERICAN</code> by default)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Org. ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Organization ID of the Oracle EBS instance</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Log Requests and Responses

To log requests and responses when using the connector, configure a logger by adding this line to
the `Loggers` element of the `log4j2.xml` configuration file for the Mule app:

```xml
<AsyncLogger name="name="org.mule.service.http.impl.service.HttpMessageLogger" level="DEBUG"/>
```

The following example shows the `Loggers` element with the `AsyncLogger` line added:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Configuration status="WARN">
	<Appenders>
	    <Console name="Console" target="SYSTEM_OUT">
	    	<PatternLayout pattern="%d{HH:mm:ss.SSS} [%t] %-5level %logger{36} - %msg%n"/>
		</Console>
	</Appenders>
	<Loggers>
		<AsyncLogger name="org.mule.service.http.impl.service.HttpMessageLogger" level="DEBUG" additivity="false">
			<appender-ref ref="Console" level="debug"/>
		</AsyncLogger>
	</Loggers>
</Configuration>
```

You can view the app log in the following ways:

- If youâre running the app from the Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line:
  - The app log is visible in your OS console.
  - Unless the log file path was customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view
    the app log in this default location:

`MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`

For more information about the app log, see
[Configuring Logging](../../mule-runtime/latest/logging-in-mule).

## Use Case: Create an Oracle EBS 12.2 Global Element

Follow the steps below to create an Oracle EBS 12.2 global element for Web Services and PL/SQL
invocation:

![Studio flow with Listener, Transform Message, and Invoke PL/SQL REST Service](_images/oracle-ebs122-flow.png)

1.  Create a new Mule Project in Studio and select an HTTP Listener as a source in the new flow.
2.  Add a new HTTP Listener global element configuration.
    1.  Specify the **Host** and **Port** parameters with the following values:

        <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>0.0.0.0</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>8081</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    2.  Click **Save**.

3.  Assign the new global configuration to your HTTP Listener.
4.  Specify the HTTP Listener path as the `/start` value.
5.  Drag a **Transform Message** element and add it the following code:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/xml
    ns ns0 http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/per/rest/hr_location_api/create_location/
    ---
    {
      ns0#InputParameters: {
        ns0#P_VALIDATE: 0,
        ns0#P_EFFECTIVE_DATE: now,
        ns0#P_LOCATION_CODE: "HR- MuleSoft Office",
        ns0#P_DESCRIPTION: "Description Office"
      }
    }
    ```

6.  Drag a new Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2 component into the flow.
7.  In the **General** tab, configure the Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2 connector global element with
    its environment values.
8.  In the properties editor of Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2 connector, set **PL/SQL** to
    `hr_location_api` and **Operation** to `CREATE_LOCATION`:

    ![Oracle Create Location operation properties window](_images/oracle-ebs122-operation1.png)

9.  Copy the following content under the **Message > Body** section:

    #\[payload\]

10. Save and run the project as a Mule application.
11. Navigate to `http://127.0.0.1:8081/start` to test the application.

## Use Case: XML

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:oracle-ebs122="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/oracle-ebs122"
xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/oracle-ebs122
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/oracle-ebs122/current/mule-oracle-ebs122.xsd">
		<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties"/>

	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" basePath="/" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<oracle-ebs122:config name="Oracle_ebs122_Config" doc:name="Oracle-ebs122 Config" >
		<oracle-ebs122:plsql-connection host="${config.host}"
		port="${config.port}" username="${config.username}"
		password="${config.password}"
		restServiceLocatorAlias="${config.restServiceLocatorAlias}"
		fndWebSecAlias="${config.fndWebSecAlias}"
		responsibility="${config.responsibility}"
		respApplication="${config.respApplication}"
		securityGroup="${config.securityGroup}"
		nlsLanguage="${config.nlsLanguage}"
		orgId="${config.orgId}"/>
	</oracle-ebs122:config>
	<flow name="oracle-ebs122Flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"
		path=â/startâ/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/xml
ns ns0 http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/per/rest/hr_location_api/create_location/
---
{
	ns0#InputParameters: {
		ns0#P_VALIDATE: 0,
		ns0#P_EFFECTIVE_DATE: now,
		ns0#P_LOCATION_CODE: "HR- MuleSoft BA",
		ns0#P_DESCRIPTION: "Buenos Aires Office"
	}
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
			<ee:variables >
				<ee:set-variable variableName="payload" ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/xml
ns ns0 http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/per/rest/hr_location_api/create_location/
---
{
	    ns0#InputParameters: {
        ns0#P_VALIDATE: 0,
        ns0#P_EFFECTIVE_DATE: now,
        ns0#P_LOCATION_CODE: 'LocCode001â,
        ns0#P_DESCRIPTION: 'Location 001â
    }
}]]></ee:set-variable>
			</ee:variables>
		</ee:transform>
		<oracle-ebs122:invoke-pl-sql-rest-service
		doc:name="Invoke PL/SQL REST Service"
		config-ref="Oracle_ebs122_Config"
		operation="CREATE_LOCATION" plSql="hr_location_api">
			<oracle-ebs122:input ><![CDATA[#[payload]]]></oracle-ebs122:input>
		</oracle-ebs122:invoke-pl-sql-rest-service>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

  [Deploying a REST Service](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E26401_01/doc.122/e20927/T511473T634173.htm)
  section
- [Oracle E-Business Suite Documentation Web Library](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E26401_01/index.htm)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Oracle EBS 12.2 Connector 2.3 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) 12.2 provides real-time connectivity to query
and update Oracle EBS using REST web services.

## Configurations

---

### Default Configuration

Default configuration for Oracle EBS 12.2 operations.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#config_plsql">Plsql</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### PL/SQL

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Hostname of the Oracle EBS instance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port where the Oracle HTTP Server listens.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SSL enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables SSL to securely communicate with Oracle EBS Web Services.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username to access the Oracle EBS REST service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password to access the Oracle EBS REST service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>REST Service Locator alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias assigned to the web service with internal name <code>oracle.apps.fnd.rep.ws.service.EbsRestLocator</code> when deployed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FND Web Sec alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias assigned to the PL/SQL web service with internal name <code>FND_WEB_SEC</code> when deployed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Responsibility name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Oracle EBS application responsibility information. Accepts responsibility_key, such as SYSTEM_ADMINISTRATOR, as its value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Responsibility application name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Responsibility application short name information. Accepts Application Short Name, such as FND, as its value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Security group name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Accepts Security Group Key, such as STANDARD, as its value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>NLS language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the language preferences, such as AMERICAN.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Org. ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the operating unit assigned to the specified responsibility. It is the only organization that the user works on.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyParameterGroup">Proxy Parameter Group</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy connection if needed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TCP Client Socket Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TcpClientSocketParameterGroup">TCP Client Socket Parameter Group</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>TCP client socket properties for the connection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Context</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>TLS context for secure connectivity.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMS Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#jms-config_jms">Jms</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#jms-config_jms-url">Jms Url</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Receive Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout, in milliseconds.</p></td><td><p>30000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### JMS

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username for the JMS connection type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password for the JMS connection type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Hostname for the connection type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port of the connection type.</p></td><td><p>1521</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Database SID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Database system identifier (SID).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### JMS URL

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username for the JMS URL connection type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password for the JMS URL connection type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL for the connection type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Associated Operations

- [Invoke PL/SQL REST Service](#invokePlSqlRestService)

## Associated Sources

- [Business Events Source](#BusinessEventsSource)

## Operations

### Invoke PL/SQL REST Service

`<oracle-ebs122:invoke-pl-sql-rest-service>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Input</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>PL/SQL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>PL/SQL name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Operation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Operation to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- ORACLE-EBS122:UNKNOWN
- ORACLE-EBS122:BAD_REQUEST
- ORACLE-EBS122:METADATA_ERROR
- ORACLE-EBS122:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ORACLE-EBS122:JMS
- ORACLE-EBS122:CONNECTIVITY
- ORACLE-EBS122:UNAUTHORIZED
- ORACLE-EBS122:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ORACLE-EBS122:REQUEST_FAILED
- ORACLE-EBS122:FORBIDDEN
- ORACLE-EBS122:XML_PARSING

## Sources

### Business Events Source

`<oracle-ebs122:business-events-source>`

> [!NOTE] The **Business Events Source** does not filter the incoming events. The user must filter
> the events after they arrive at the source.  
> The **Consume** operation in Oracleâs Advanced Queuing API library, which the connector uses to
> connect with EBS, may override the `AQ$_WF_BPEL_QTAB_S` field in the subscriber table. The
> **Business Events Source** calls the **Consume** operation internally.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Durable Subscription</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set this value to <code>true</code> to create a durable subscription for the topic.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the durable subscription that already exists in the EBS instance.</p></td><td><p><code>muleBusinessEvents</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter message criteria</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters messages based on SQL like criteria.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a redelivery policy to any source in a flow.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#Event">Event</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [JMS Configuration](#jms-config)

## Types

### Proxy Parameter Group

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host for the type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username for the type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password for the type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TCP Client Socket Parameter Group

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Send Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receive Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Tcp No Delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Linger</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keep Alive</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how to validate certificates.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Settig this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector operation source or operation
attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often the Mule app attempts to reconnect to the remote service or API, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before
Mule considers it eligible for expiration.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before it is considered eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the memory stream. If the stream data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Event

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Correlation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Correlation ID for a message. The value set for the correlation ID is used as the item key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines if errors occur when processing the event.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Stack</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Locates the source of an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Subscription</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Subscription that was executed when an error is thrown.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Event Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Data that describes what occurs in the event.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Event Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique identifier for the instance of the event.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Event Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the event.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Agent</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Agent">Agent</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Agent from which the event is sent. It is initially set to <code>null</code> for locally raised events.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter List</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of parameters and their values.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Priority</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Describes the priority of the message recipient dequeuing the message. <code>1</code> corresponds to a high priority while <code>99</code> corresponds to a low priority.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receive Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Shows the date and time of when the message is dequeued by an agent listener.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Represents the date and time when the message can be dequeued.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Agent</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Agent">Agent</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Agent to which the event is sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Agent

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the agent.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>System</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>System where the agent is located.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a delivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message was redelivered. This property can be set only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
